top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The day was dominated by a possible end to the US-Iran war: Washington says a peace memorandum could be signed on June 14 in Geneva and Trump called off planned strikes, though Tehran insists nothing is finalised. Markets cheered the de-escalation, with stocks rallying and oil sinking to two-month lows, while Elon Musk's SpaceX priced a record US$75 billion IPO. Elsewhere, the 2026 World Cup kicked off in Mexico, South Korea jailed ex-president Yoon for 30 years, and an Ebola outbreak kept spreading in DR Congo.

stories.headline: US-Iran peace memorandum could be signed June 14 in Geneva, source says
stories.summary: A source says the US and Iran could sign a peace memorandum on June 14 in Geneva to end their war, after Trump called off fresh strikes following talks. Iran says no final decision has been made and Tehran insists nothing is finalised; the BBC reports the deal is not yet confirmed.

stories.headline: Stocks rally, oil hits two-month lows on Gulf de-escalation hopes
stories.summary: Equity markets rallied and oil prices fell to two-month lows after Trump called off fresh Iran strikes and word spread of a possible US-Iran deal. The pullback in crude eased fears of a supply shock through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Musk's SpaceX prices record US$75 billion IPO at US$135 a share
stories.summary: SpaceX priced what would be a record US$75 billion initial public offering at US$135 a share, in one of the largest listings ever. The float underscores investor appetite for Elon Musk's rocket-and-satellite empire.

stories.headline: 2026 World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, dancing and protests
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup opened in Mexico City with a ceremony featuring Shakira and Andrea Bocelli, as debutant nations celebrated. Some protests accompanied the festivities, and US security officials said they are on alert for 'lone wolf' attacks across host cities.

stories.headline: South Korea's ex-president Yoon Suk Yeol jailed 30 years over North Korea drone incident
stories.summary: A South Korean court sentenced former president Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in jail over a North Korea drone incident, a landmark ruling against the ousted leader. It marks one of the heaviest sentences handed to a former South Korean head of state.

stories.headline: Philippine earthquake death toll rises to 55 as rain, aftershocks hamper rescue
stories.summary: The death toll from a Philippine earthquake climbed to 55, with rain and aftershocks slowing rescue efforts. Authorities continue to search collapsed structures for survivors.

stories.headline: Thai King's eldest daughter Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies, aged 47
stories.summary: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha, the king's eldest daughter, has died aged 47 after a long illness; she had been in a coma since collapsing more than three years ago. The palace announced her death, prompting national mourning.

stories.headline: Ebola spreading into new areas in northeast DR Congo, WHO says
stories.summary: The WHO says the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak is spreading into new areas of northeastern DR Congo, including a crowded displacement camp, and warns 'blind spots' may be hiding its full extent. The latest WHO figures count 534 confirmed cases and 93 deaths across DR Congo and Uganda as of June 6.

stories.headline: UK defence minister John Healey quits with stinging rebuke of PM Starmer
stories.summary: British defence minister John Healey resigned, delivering a pointed rebuke of Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The departure adds to political pressure on Starmer's government.

stories.headline: China arrests US scholar of Myanmar on suspicion of spying
stories.summary: China said it arrested a US-based scholar of Myanmar, Min Zin, on suspicion of spying, a move likely to further strain US-China ties. Beijing gave limited details about the allegations.

stories.headline: EU overhaul to toughen migration rules takes effect amid doubts over impact
stories.summary: A long-debated EU overhaul tightening migration and asylum rules came into force, though officials and analysts question how much it will change in practice. The package centralises some asylum procedures across member states.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A grim maritime accident led the day, with three people killed after a supply boat sank following a collision off Pasir Panjang Terminal. On the wallet front, household refuse-collection fees rise from July 1 — the first increase in two years — while COE car premiums stayed near record highs. PM Wong will give his National Day Rally on Aug 23, MRT reliability is near a record high, and the data watchdog is probing a cyber breach at tuition operator Global Schools Group. A rare cold snap (a low of 20.1°C) had Singaporeans reaching for sweaters.

stories.headline: Three dead after supply boat sinks following collision off Pasir Panjang Terminal
stories.summary: Three people died after a supply boat sank following a collision off Pasir Panjang Terminal on June 12. Authorities mounted a search-and-rescue operation; the incident is under investigation.

stories.headline: Household refuse collection fees to rise from July 1 — first increase in two years
stories.summary: Household waste-collection fees will go up from July 1, the first increase in two years, affecting HDB flats, condos and landed homes. The revision reflects higher collection and disposal costs.

stories.headline: PM Wong to deliver National Day Rally speech on Aug 23
stories.summary: Prime Minister Lawrence Wong will deliver his National Day Rally speech on Aug 23, the government announced. It will be a key address setting out policy priorities for the year ahead.

stories.headline: PDPC to investigate cybersecurity incident at Global Schools Group
stories.summary: The Personal Data Protection Commission will investigate a cybersecurity incident at tuition and education operator Global Schools Group. The probe will look at whether personal data was adequately protected.

stories.headline: MRT reliability nears record high as Circle Line performance improves 50%
stories.summary: MRT reliability is near a record high, with the Circle Line posting the biggest improvement in May, LTA figures show. Rail reliability stayed above the 2-million train-km benchmark between delays.

stories.headline: Six Hyundai Engineering & Construction employees charged over alleged corruption
stories.summary: Six employees of Hyundai Engineering & Construction were charged over alleged graft tied to a Labrador substation project, in a case linked to workplace-safety matters. The charges were heard in court on June 12.

stories.headline: Man charged with molesting, filming 14 women at Holland Village massage parlour
stories.summary: A 52-year-old man was charged with molesting and taking intimate videos of 14 women at a Holland Village massage parlour. He faces multiple charges following police investigations.

stories.headline: Singapore deepens AI safety push with IMDA-Microsoft partnership
stories.summary: Singapore's IMDA signed a partnership with Microsoft to advance frontier AI safety, including testing and risk-evaluation work. It is part of a broader national push to develop and govern AI responsibly.

stories.headline: Sweater weather: Singapore temperature falls to 20.1°C, lowest so far in 2026
stories.summary: Singapore recorded a low of 20.1°C, the coolest reading so far in 2026, prompting many to reach for jackets and sweaters. NEA attributed the chill to recent wet, windy conditions.

stories.headline: Mum of five nearly misses S$30,000 Sheng Siong prize, thinking call was a scam
stories.summary: A Singaporean mother of five nearly missed a S$30,000 Sheng Siong lucky-draw prize because she mistook the notification call for a scam. She eventually claimed the windfall after being convinced it was genuine.

stories.headline: Mandopop icons JJ Lin and Jay Chou drop surprise duet
stories.summary: Mandopop stars JJ Lin and Jay Chou surprised fans with an unannounced duet, sending social media into a frenzy. Listeners were quick to ask for more collaborations from the pair.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The US-Iran war appears to be de-escalating sharply: Trump called off planned strikes and Washington floated signing a peace memorandum in Geneva on June 14, though Tehran says nothing is final. In Ukraine, Kyiv is pressing deep strikes — disrupting water in Russian-held Donetsk — while seeking a further US$20 billion from allies. The Israel-Lebanon front stays tense, with Hezbollah hoping any US-Iran deal extends to Lebanon after deadly Israeli strikes in the south.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran
theatres.status: De-escalating — Trump called off fresh strikes; US says a peace memorandum could be signed June 14 in Geneva, but Iran says nothing is finalised

timeline.event: Satellite imagery indicates more than 50 Iranian military bases have been damaged in US strikes since the war began, the BBC reports.
Trump says he will hit Iran 'very hard' on Thursday night, before later calling the planned strikes off following talks.
Iran says no final decision has been made on a deal Trump hopes could be signed soon; Tehran tells the BBC nothing is finalised.
US stocks rally and oil falls to two-month lows after Trump calls off fresh Iran strikes amid talk of a deal.
A source says a US-Iran peace memorandum could be signed on June 14 in Geneva.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine
theatres.status: Ongoing — Ukraine pressing deep strikes into Russian-held territory and seeking a further US$20 billion from allies

timeline.event: A Ukrainian strike disrupts water supply in the Russia-held Donetsk region, Interfax reports.
The Russian city of Nizhnekamsk cancels public events after a drone threat.
Ukraine will ask allies for US$20 billion to keep up its momentum against Russia, a source says.

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon/Gaza
theatres.status: Tense — Israeli strikes continue in southern Lebanon; ceasefire hopes are tied to the US-Iran track

timeline.event: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, according to reports.
US Vice-President Vance says Israeli PM Netanyahu 'has got some things wrong'.
A Hezbollah politician says the group is confident any US-Iran deal will include Lebanon.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The dominant outbreak is Bundibugyo-strain Ebola in DR Congo and Uganda, which is escalating fast: the WHO's latest figures count 534 confirmed cases and 93 deaths (case-fatality rate ~17%) as of June 6, and the virus is now spreading into new areas and a crowded displacement camp — the WHO warns 'blind spots' may be hiding the true scale. The separate MV Hondius cruise-ship hantavirus cluster (13 cases, 3 deaths) appears contained, with no new WHO update since late May.

outbreaks.name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Escalating; spreading into new areas and a displacement camp, WHO warns of undercounting
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed cases only, DR Congo + Uganda combined (DRC 515 cases/91 deaths; Uganda the remainder), CFR 17.4%, per WHO DON606 (published 8 Jun). Wire reports of further spread on 12 Jun are not yet reflected in a formal WHO DON.

outbreaks.name: Hantavirus — MV Hondius cruise-ship cluster
outbreaks.status: Appears contained; figures stale — no WHO update since 28 May (DON604)
outbreaks.scope_note: 11 confirmed + 2 probable cases, 3 deaths, among passengers/crew of multiple nationalities (incl. Canada, Netherlands, Spain), per WHO DON604. Figures are now more than two weeks old; treat as a wind-down rather than active escalation.

stories.headline: Ebola spreading into new areas in northeast DR Congo, WHO says
stories.summary: The WHO says the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak is moving into new areas of northeastern DR Congo. Health teams are racing to trace contacts and contain spread amid limited access.

stories.headline: Ebola outbreak spreads to crowded displacement camp in Congo
stories.summary: The Ebola outbreak has reached a crowded displacement camp in Congo, raising fears of faster transmission among vulnerable people. Aid groups warn that conditions in such camps make containment harder.

stories.headline: 'Blind spots' could hide full spread of Congo's Ebola outbreak, WHO suggests
stories.summary: The WHO suggests 'blind spots' in surveillance could be hiding the full extent of Congo's Ebola outbreak, meaning case counts may understate the true picture. It is urging stronger testing and access in affected zones.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Patch-now items dominate this cycle: a max-severity Ivanti Sentry flaw (CVE-2026-10520, CVSS 10.0) is under active attack with CISA giving agencies days to patch, ShinyHunters is exploiting an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273) to breach universities, and Microsoft shipped a record 206 fixes including three zero-days. AI tooling is a fresh battleground — researchers showed 'agentjacking' and other tricks that make AI coding agents run malicious code, alongside exploited flaws in Langflow and LangGraph. A heavy run of breaches (Novo Nordisk, a Japanese energy firm with 10.9M records, Nottingham University) and law-enforcement takedowns round out the week.

stories.headline: Agentjacking attack tricks AI coding agents into running malicious code
stories.summary: Researchers describe 'agentjacking', a new class of attack that tricks AI coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines. It highlights growing supply-chain-style risks as AI agents gain the ability to execute commands.

stories.headline: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical-trials data
stories.summary: Novo Nordisk disclosed a security breach affecting clinical-trials data. The drugmaker is investigating the scope and notifying affected parties.

stories.headline: LangGraph flaw chain exposes self-hosted AI agents to remote code execution
stories.summary: A chain of flaws in LangGraph can expose self-hosted AI agents to remote code execution. Operators of self-hosted deployments are urged to update and review their configurations.

stories.headline: INTERPOL operation takes down Sniper Dz phishing platform, arrests admin
stories.summary: An INTERPOL-coordinated operation dismantled the Sniper Dz phishing-as-a-service platform and arrested its administrator. The takedown removes a tool widely used to build phishing pages.

stories.headline: Max-severity Ivanti Sentry flaw under active attack; CISA orders fast patching
stories.summary: A maximum-severity OS command-injection flaw in Ivanti Sentry (CVE-2026-10520, CVSS 10.0) is being exploited in attacks. CISA added it to its KEV catalog and gave federal agencies days to patch.

stories.headline: Tchap messenger breach affects over 73,000 French government employees
stories.summary: The French government said a breach of its Tchap messaging app affected more than 73,000 accounts. Officials are assessing what data was exposed.

stories.headline: Europol disrupts 'AudiA6' crypto-laundering service used by ransomware gangs
stories.summary: Authorities led by Europol dismantled 'AudiA6', a cryptocurrency-laundering service used by ransomware gangs to cash out. The action disrupts a key part of the ransomware money-laundering pipeline.

stories.headline: Japanese energy firm loses drive with data of 10.9 million clients
stories.summary: A Japanese energy company reported losing a storage drive containing data on 10.9 million clients. The firm is investigating and warning affected customers.

stories.headline: ShinyHunters exploits Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day to breach universities
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters group is exploiting an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273, CVSS 9.8) to breach universities and steal data. Oracle has issued mitigations; organisations are urged to apply them urgently.

stories.headline: New GreatXML exploit bypasses Windows BitLocker via recovery-partition XML
stories.summary: A new technique dubbed GreatXML can bypass Windows BitLocker using crafted XML files in the recovery partition. Microsoft has issued a related BitLocker recovery fix for Windows Server 2025.

stories.headline: The Gentlemen ransomware claims 478 victims, can spread like a worm
stories.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware operation claims 478 victims and has worm-like self-spreading capabilities, researchers say. Its ability to propagate automatically raises the risk of fast, wide intrusions.

stories.headline: OceanLotus hits Vietnam investors with SPECTRALVIPER in FireAnt campaign
stories.summary: The OceanLotus group targeted Vietnamese investors with the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor in a campaign tracked as FireAnt. The activity reflects continued espionage-style targeting in the region.

stories.headline: Nottingham University data breach affects over 450,000 students
stories.summary: A data breach at Nottingham University affects more than 450,000 students, BleepingComputer reports. The university is investigating and notifying those impacted.

stories.headline: GitHub to disable npm install scripts by default to curb supply-chain attacks
stories.summary: GitHub will disable npm install scripts by default to reduce supply-chain attacks that abuse them to run code during installation. It is a notable hardening change for the JavaScript ecosystem.

stories.headline: Ivanti, Fortinet and SAP release patches for multiple critical vulnerabilities
stories.summary: Ivanti, Fortinet and SAP released patches addressing multiple critical vulnerabilities across their products. Administrators are advised to prioritise the critical fixes.

stories.headline: Langflow flaw CVE-2026-5027 exploited for unauthenticated RCE
stories.summary: A path-traversal flaw in the AI dev platform Langflow (CVE-2026-5027) is being exploited for unauthenticated remote code execution. Users are urged to update affected installations.

stories.headline: CISA adds Cisco, Chrome and Arista flaws to KEV catalog amid active exploitation
stories.summary: CISA added actively exploited Cisco, Google Chrome (V8 zero-day CVE-2026-11645) and Arista flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Agencies and organisations should patch these immediately.

stories.headline: Microsoft patches record 206 flaws, including three zero-days
stories.summary: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday fixed a record 206 flaws, including three zero-days and several critical remote-code-execution bugs. The unusually large update underscores the urgency of prompt patching.

stories.headline: ServiceNow flaw exploited to gain unauthorized access to customer instances
stories.summary: Attackers exploited a ServiceNow flaw to gain unauthorized access to customer instances. Organisations using the platform are urged to review configurations and apply fixes.

stories.headline: Microsoft Defender 'RoguePlanet' zero-day grants SYSTEM access on updated Windows
stories.summary: A Microsoft Defender zero-day dubbed RoguePlanet can grant SYSTEM-level access even on fully updated Windows, researchers report. It would let attackers escalate privileges on compromised machines.

stories.headline: Six 'Proto6' vulnerabilities in protobuf.js expose Node.js apps to RCE and DoS
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed six 'Proto6' vulnerabilities in protobuf.js that can expose Node.js applications to remote code execution and denial of service. Developers should update the library.

stories.headline: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE flaw lets domain users run remote code
stories.summary: A remote-code-execution flaw in Veeam Backup & Replication lets authenticated domain users run code remotely. Given backups are a prime ransomware target, admins are urged to patch quickly.
